name: CIS Critical Security Controls v8
description: The CIS Critical Security Controls (CIS Controls) are a prioritized set of actions that collectively form a defense-in-depth set of best practices to mitigate the most common cyber attacks. CIS Controls v8 consolidates the prior 20 controls into 18 and reorganizes them around modern enterprise activities. Implementation Groups (IG1/IG2/IG3) provide a prioritized adoption path.

domains.name: CIS Control 1: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
domains.description: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all enterprise assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments, to accurately know the totality of assets that need to be monitored and protected.

controls.name: Establish and maintain detailed enterprise asset inventory
controls.question: Does the organization maintain a detailed, accurate, and current inventory of all enterprise assets including hardware, software, cloud resources, and IoT/OT devices?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Asset inventory spreadsheet or CMDB, network discovery scan output, MDM enrollment list, cloud asset export (AWS Config, Azure Resource Inventory), last update date.
controls.why_it_matters: You cannot protect what you don't know exists. Unknown assets are unmanaged, unpatched, and commonly exploited.
common_weaknesses: Spreadsheet-based manual inventory that is out of date
No process to reconcile inventory against actual network devices
controls.remediation_guidance: Implement automated asset discovery (e.g., Lansweeper, network scanners, cloud-native inventory tools). Reconcile against discovered assets monthly. Include all endpoint types.
controls.risk_impact: Unmanaged devices with known vulnerabilities used as attack entry points.

scoring_guidance.4: Real-time inventory with unauthorized device detection

controls.name: Address unauthorized assets
controls.question: Does the organization have a process to detect unauthorized or unmanaged devices connecting to the network, and take action (isolate or remove) within a defined timeframe?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: NAC (Network Access Control) configuration, 802.1X deployment evidence, unauthorized device detection alerts, response procedures, DHCP logs reviewed for unknown devices.
controls.why_it_matters: Attackers frequently leverage unmanaged BYOD devices or rogue hardware to establish a foothold on the network.
common_weaknesses: Unknown devices visible on network but not investigated
No alerting on new device connections
controls.remediation_guidance: Deploy Network Access Control (NAC) to enforce device authentication before network access is granted. Alert on unauthorized devices. Implement 802.1X for wired and wireless networks.
controls.risk_impact: Unauthorized devices used for network reconnaissance, lateral movement, and data exfiltration.

scoring_guidance.3: NAC deployed for wireless; alerts on unknown devices
scoring_guidance.4: 802.1X on all wired and wireless; automated quarantine of unauthorized devices

domains.name: CIS Control 2: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
domains.description: Actively manage all software on the network so that only authorized software is installed and can execute, and unauthorized and unmanaged software is found and prevented from installation or execution.

controls.name: Establish and maintain software inventory
controls.question: Does the organization maintain an inventory of all authorized software, including version numbers, vendor, and end-of-life dates?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Software inventory, approved software list, patch management platform showing installed software, SaaS subscription inventory, EOL tracking document.
controls.why_it_matters: Unauthorized or outdated software introduces vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit.
controls.remediation_guidance: Maintain an authorized software list. Use endpoint management tools to enumerate installed software. Establish a process to track SaaS subscriptions. Flag EOL software for replacement.
controls.risk_impact: Unpatched software exploited; unauthorized tools expose data; EOL systems with no available patches.

scoring_guidance.3: Full inventory with versions and EOL status
scoring_guidance.4: Automated inventory with policy enforcement and unauthorized software alerting

controls.name: Allowlist authorized software
controls.question: Does the organization use application allowlisting (whitelisting) or equivalent controls to prevent unauthorized software from executing on endpoints?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Application control policy configuration (AppLocker, Windows Defender Application Control, Bit9/Carbon Black), test results showing unauthorized software blocked.
controls.why_it_matters: Application allowlisting is one of the most effective controls against malware execution, including fileless attacks and living-off-the-land techniques.
common_weaknesses: Policy too permissive (all executables in Program Files allowed)
controls.remediation_guidance: Implement application allowlisting using Microsoft WDAC, AppLocker, or a commercial tool. Start with audit mode to identify gaps before enforcement. Prioritize high-risk users and systems.
controls.risk_impact: Malware execution on endpoints; unauthorized tools used for data exfiltration or lateral movement.

scoring_guidance.4: Allowlisting enforced across all endpoints with exception management process

domains.name: CIS Control 3: Data Protection
domains.description: Develop processes and technical controls to identify, classify, securely handle, retain, and dispose of data.

controls.name: Establish and maintain data management process
controls.question: Does the organization have a documented data management process that covers data classification, handling, retention, and secure disposal?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Data classification policy, data retention schedule, data disposal/destruction procedures and records, data flow diagrams.
controls.why_it_matters: Without data governance, organizations cannot apply proportionate security controls, retain data appropriately, or dispose of it securely.
common_weaknesses: Sensitive data discovered in unexpected locations
controls.remediation_guidance: Define a data classification policy. Establish retention schedules by data type. Implement a secure disposal process for physical media (shredding, degaussing) and digital data (DoD 5220.22-M or AES encrypt-and-delete).
controls.risk_impact: Data breach exposure; regulatory violations for improper retention; improperly disposed media leading to data recovery.

scoring_guidance.4: Technical controls enforcing classification, retention, and disposal with audit trails

controls.name: Encrypt data on end-user devices
controls.question: Is data on end-user devices (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) encrypted with full-disk encryption?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: BitLocker/FileVault deployment report, MDM encryption compliance report, mobile device encryption policy and MDM enforcement.
controls.why_it_matters: Full-disk encryption protects data when a device is lost or stolen, preventing unauthorized access to sensitive data.
common_weaknesses: Mobile devices not enrolled in MDM with encryption enforced
controls.remediation_guidance: Enable BitLocker (Windows) or FileVault (Mac) on all laptops. Enforce encryption via MDM for mobile devices. Store recovery keys securely in an identity management system.
controls.risk_impact: Lost or stolen device leads to data breach; regulatory notification obligations triggered.

scoring_guidance.3: All laptops and mobile devices encrypted via MDM
scoring_guidance.4: Full-disk encryption enforced everywhere with centralized key management

domains.name: CIS Control 5: Account Management
domains.description: Use processes and tools to assign and manage authorization to credentials for user accounts, including administrator accounts, as well as service accounts, to enterprise assets and software.

controls.name: Establish and maintain inventory of accounts
controls.question: Does the organization maintain an up-to-date inventory of all user, service, and administrator accounts, including their authorized purpose?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Active Directory or IdP account export, service account inventory, privileged account list, date of last account review.
controls.why_it_matters: Untracked accounts, especially privileged and service accounts, are frequently compromised and used for lateral movement.
common_weaknesses: Former employee accounts discovered months after termination
controls.remediation_guidance: Export all accounts from Active Directory, Azure AD, cloud platforms, and SaaS tools. Review quarterly. Disable or remove accounts that cannot be attributed to a current user or system function.
controls.risk_impact: Orphaned accounts exploited post-compromise; service account credentials used for lateral movement.

scoring_guidance.4: Automated account lifecycle management with real-time inventory

controls.name: Disable dormant accounts
controls.question: Does the organization identify and disable or remove accounts that have been inactive for a defined period (e.g., 30–45 days)?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Last logon report from AD/IdP, dormant account policy, evidence of disabled/removed inactive accounts.
controls.why_it_matters: Dormant accounts represent an attack surface that may go undetected when compromised, as there is no legitimate user to notice unusual activity.
common_weaknesses: Dormant accounts exist for years without review
Remote/service accounts exempt from dormancy policy
controls.remediation_guidance: Configure automated detection of accounts inactive for > 30 days. Disable after warning notification. Remove after 60 days of inactivity (with manager approval exception process).
controls.risk_impact: Dormant accounts compromised without detection; credential stuffing against forgotten accounts.

scoring_guidance.4: Automated disable/remove with exception workflow and audit trail

domains.name: CIS Control 6: Access Control Management
domains.description: Use processes and tools to create, assign, manage, and revoke access credentials and privileges for user, administrator, and service accounts for enterprise assets and software.

controls.name: Require MFA for all user accounts
controls.question: Is multi-factor authentication (MFA) required for all user accounts across all applications, particularly for remote access, email, and cloud services?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: MFA enrollment report, Conditional Access or equivalent policy export, VPN MFA configuration, evidence of MFA enforcement (not just availability).
controls.why_it_matters: MFA prevents account takeover even when credentials are compromised — which happens constantly via phishing, credential stuffing, and data breaches.
common_weaknesses: MFA not required for on-premises applications
controls.remediation_guidance: Enforce MFA via Conditional Access policies. Block legacy authentication. Require phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2) for administrators and privileged users.
controls.risk_impact: Account takeover, business email compromise, unauthorized access to cloud resources.

scoring_guidance.3: MFA enforced for all users on remote access and cloud
scoring_guidance.4: MFA enforced everywhere; phishing-resistant MFA for privileged accounts; legacy auth blocked

controls.name: Define and maintain role-based access control
controls.question: Does the organization use role-based access control (RBAC) to define and enforce access rights based on job function and least privilege?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: RBAC documentation, role definitions, access request/approval workflow, evidence of access reviews, separation of duties matrix.
controls.why_it_matters: RBAC reduces over-provisioning and limits the damage an attacker can do with a compromised account.
common_weaknesses: Access granted ad hoc without RBAC
Role definitions not reviewed after organizational changes
Access accumulated over time without removal
controls.remediation_guidance: Define roles aligned to job functions. Implement RBAC in Active Directory/IdP. Conduct semi-annual access reviews to confirm role assignments remain appropriate.
controls.risk_impact: Over-privileged accounts with broad access; massive blast radius if compromised.

scoring_guidance.4: Automated RBAC enforcement with joiner/mover/leaver integration

domains.name: CIS Control 7: Continuous Vulnerability Management
domains.description: Develop a plan to continuously assess and track vulnerabilities on all enterprise assets, so that the risk can be addressed and the window of opportunity for attackers is minimized.

controls.name: Establish and maintain vulnerability management process
controls.question: Does the organization have a documented vulnerability management process with defined scan frequency, remediation SLAs, and exception handling?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Vulnerability management policy, scan schedule, recent scan reports, remediation tracking evidence, SLA compliance metrics.
controls.why_it_matters: Attackers exploit known vulnerabilities, often within days of public disclosure. A structured VM program closes the window of exploitation.
common_weaknesses: Scans run but results not tracked to remediation
No SLAs for patching based on severity
controls.remediation_guidance: Define a VM policy with scan frequency (at least quarterly; monthly preferred) and remediation SLAs (Critical: 72h, High: 14 days, Medium: 30 days). Track findings to closure.
controls.risk_impact: Known vulnerabilities exploited; ransomware deployment via unpatched systems.

scoring_guidance.3: Defined process with SLAs and tracking
scoring_guidance.4: Continuous scanning with automated remediation ticketing and SLA compliance reporting

domains.name: CIS Control 8: Audit Log Management
domains.description: Collect, alert, review, and retain audit logs of events that could help detect, understand, or recover from an attack.

controls.name: Establish and maintain audit log management process
controls.question: Does the organization collect, centralize, and retain audit logs from critical systems for a minimum of 90 days (1 year preferred), and is log integrity protected?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Log management policy, SIEM or centralized log platform evidence, log retention configuration, protected log storage evidence, examples of log review.
controls.why_it_matters: Logs are essential for detecting attacks in progress and conducting post-incident forensics. Without logs, you are blind.
common_weaknesses: Logs not centralized (siloed on individual systems)
Log integrity not protected (logs could be tampered with)
controls.remediation_guidance: Deploy a SIEM or centralized log management platform. Collect logs from firewalls, servers, endpoints, cloud, and identity systems. Set retention to at least 90 days online and 1 year archived. Enable log integrity controls.
controls.risk_impact: Inability to detect attacks; forensic investigation impossible; compliance violations (PCI DSS, HIPAA require audit logging).

scoring_guidance.3: Centralized logs, 90+ day retention, integrity protected
scoring_guidance.4: Full SIEM with alerting, 1-year retention, regular review, and forensics capability

domains.name: CIS Control 9: Email and Web Browser Protections
domains.description: Improve protections and detections of threats from email and web vectors, as these are opportunities for attackers to manipulate human behavior through direct engagement.

controls.name: Ensure use of supported browsers and email clients
controls.question: Does the organization ensure that only supported, up-to-date web browsers and email clients are used, and are browser-based attacks mitigated through configuration hardening?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Browser inventory and versions, browser update policy, browser extension management policy, email client version report.
controls.why_it_matters: Browsers and email clients are the primary attack vector for phishing and drive-by download attacks.
common_weaknesses: Browser plugins like Java, Flash still installed
controls.remediation_guidance: Enforce browser updates via GPO or MDM. Restrict browser extension installation to an approved list. Remove legacy plugins (Java, Flash). Consider browser isolation for high-risk use cases.
controls.risk_impact: Drive-by malware installation, credential harvesting via browser extensions, phishing exploitation.

scoring_guidance.4: Managed browsers with allowlisted extensions, no legacy plugins, and browser isolation for sensitive access

controls.name: Block unauthorized email attachments and links
controls.question: Does the organization use email security controls to block malicious attachments, scan URLs, and prevent phishing emails from reaching end users?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Email security gateway configuration (SEG, Microsoft Defender for Office 365, Proofpoint), anti-phishing policy, attachment sandboxing evidence, URL rewriting evidence, spam filter statistics.
controls.why_it_matters: Email remains the #1 initial attack vector for malware and phishing. Layered email security controls significantly reduce successful phishing attacks.
common_weaknesses: No advanced email security (basic spam filter only)
controls.remediation_guidance: Deploy an advanced email security gateway with attachment sandboxing, URL rewriting and scanning, anti-spoofing (DMARC, DKIM, SPF), and anti-impersonation controls.
controls.risk_impact: Successful phishing, BEC attacks, malware delivery via email, ransomware.

scoring_guidance.3: Full SEG with sandboxing, URL scanning, anti-spoofing
scoring_guidance.4: Advanced email security with AI-based detection, DMARC enforcement, and security awareness integration

domains.name: CIS Control 11: Data Recovery
domains.description: Establish and maintain data recovery practices sufficient to restore in-scope enterprise assets to a pre-incident and trusted state.

controls.name: Establish and maintain data recovery process
controls.question: Does the organization have a documented, tested data backup and recovery process with defined recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO)?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Backup policy, backup schedule and coverage documentation, most recent restore test records, RTO/RPO documentation, offsite or cloud backup evidence.
controls.why_it_matters: Reliable backups are the only defense against ransomware that guarantees recovery without paying a ransom.
common_weaknesses: Backups exist but restores never tested
Backup media stored in the same location as production (destroyed in same incident)
Backups connected to the network (ransomware can encrypt them)
controls.remediation_guidance: Follow the 3-2-1 backup rule: 3 copies, 2 different media, 1 offsite/cloud. Ensure at least one copy is air-gapped or immutable. Test restore quarterly. Define and document RTOs and RPOs for critical systems.
controls.risk_impact: Complete data loss following ransomware attack; extended business downtime; financial and reputational catastrophe.

scoring_guidance.4: Immutable/air-gapped backups with defined RTOs/RPOs tested annually; automated restore verification

domains.name: CIS Control 12: Network Infrastructure Management
domains.description: Establish, implement, and actively manage network devices, in order to prevent attackers from exploiting vulnerable network services and access points.

controls.name: Ensure network infrastructure is up to date
controls.question: Are network infrastructure devices (firewalls, routers, switches, VPN concentrators) running current, supported firmware with the latest security patches applied?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Network device inventory with firmware versions, patch management records for network devices, network device EOL/EOS status check.
controls.why_it_matters: Network devices with vulnerabilities are high-value targets that provide attackers with network-wide access if compromised.
common_weaknesses: No patch management process for network devices
EOL network equipment still in production
controls.remediation_guidance: Inventory all network devices with firmware versions. Establish a patch cycle for network infrastructure. Replace EOL equipment. Change default credentials. Enable secure management protocols only (SSH, HTTPS).
controls.risk_impact: Network device compromise enabling traffic interception, routing manipulation, and bypass of security controls.

scoring_guidance.3: All devices inventoried with regular patching
scoring_guidance.4: Automated patch compliance with configuration drift detection

controls.name: Establish and maintain secure network architecture
controls.question: Does the organization implement network segmentation to isolate critical systems, separate user traffic from administrative traffic, and prevent lateral movement?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Network diagram, VLAN configuration, firewall ruleset (segmentation rules), evidence of DMZ for internet-facing systems, server network separation.
controls.why_it_matters: Flat, unsegmented networks allow attackers to move laterally from any compromised device to every other device. Segmentation contains breaches.
common_weaknesses: Workstations can communicate directly with servers
Guest Wi-Fi on same network as corporate systems
controls.remediation_guidance: Implement network segmentation using VLANs. Separate servers, user workstations, guest networks, and management traffic. Implement a DMZ for internet-facing services. Use firewall rules to enforce segmentation.
controls.risk_impact: Lateral movement from any compromised endpoint to critical systems; ransomware spreading network-wide.

scoring_guidance.4: Micro-segmentation or zero trust with east-west traffic inspection

domains.name: CIS Control 14: Security Awareness and Skills Training
domains.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program to influence behavior among the workforce to be security conscious and properly skilled to reduce cybersecurity risks.

controls.name: Establish and maintain security awareness program
controls.question: Does the organization operate a formal security awareness program that includes annual training for all employees, phishing simulations, and role-specific training for high-risk roles?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Training platform evidence, completion records, phishing simulation results and trends, curriculum outline, role-specific training for finance/executives/IT.
controls.why_it_matters: Humans remain the most commonly exploited attack vector. A mature awareness program measurably reduces susceptibility to phishing and social engineering.
common_weaknesses: Generic training not tailored to organization
controls.remediation_guidance: Implement an LMS-based awareness training platform. Require completion annually. Add role-specific modules for finance (BEC), executives (whaling), and IT (privileged access). Run quarterly phishing simulations with auto-enrollment in remedial training for clickers.
controls.risk_impact: Phishing, BEC, and social engineering attacks succeed at high rates; insider negligence incidents.

scoring_guidance.3: Annual training > 90% completion with phishing simulations
scoring_guidance.4: Continuous program with role-specific training, phishing simulations, metrics, and demonstrated behavior change

domains.name: CIS Control 17: Incident Response Management
domains.description: Establish a program to develop and maintain an incident response capability to prepare, detect, contain, and recover from attacks.

controls.name: Designate personnel to manage incident handling
controls.question: Has the organization designated an incident response team with defined roles, contact information, and escalation procedures for cybersecurity incidents?
controls.evidence_guidance: Request: Incident response plan identifying team members, contact list, evidence of incident response retainer (if using third-party IR firm), tabletop exercise records.
controls.why_it_matters: When an incident occurs, there is no time to determine who should do what. Pre-designated roles and practiced procedures dramatically improve response.
common_weaknesses: Incident response handled solely by IT with no security expertise
controls.remediation_guidance: Designate an incident response team including IT, security, legal, HR, and communications. Contract a cybersecurity incident response firm for retainer/on-call support. Test via annual tabletop exercise.
controls.risk_impact: Disorganized incident response; prolonged breach dwell time; missed legal notification deadlines; regulatory penalties.

scoring_guidance.3: IRP with designated team and annual tabletop
scoring_guidance.4: Mature IR program with retainer, tested procedures, lessons-learned process
